Therefore, a bounce back above the current trough is very likely. However, there is also a discernible downward macro trend over the last 12 months as the Fed transitions toward a looser policy stance, which tempers the probability slightly. Due to the high volatility of the series, both mean reversion and random walk behavior play a significant role. Following instructions, the probability for the farther date (Jan 2026) is pulled closer to 0.5 compared to the nearer date (Nov 2025).
